# UC-18.4.2 · Cisco Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller (NDFC) Intent-Based Configuration Compliance and Per-Switch Drift Detection

## Description

Monitors **Cisco Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller** intent baselines versus **NX-OS** running configuration for fabrics built from **VXLAN EVPN**, **MSD**, **Classic LAN**, **External Fabric**, **IPFM**, or **custom templates**—tracking **`complianceStatus`** enums (**In-Sync**, **Pending Config**, **Out-of-Sync**, **Failed**, **NA**), **`Pending Config`** versus **`Out-of-Sync`** drift indicators, **top-down** **Layer 2**/**Layer 3** network and **VRF** pushes, **POAP** bootstrap milestones, **SUM**/**ISSU** image-management workflows, **Performance Manager** statistics separate from **NDI**, underlay cabling expectations versus discovered topology, and **backup**/**restore** job outcomes sourced from **`/jobs`** plus **`/compliance-status`** **REST** responses normalized into **`cisco:ndfc:*`** sourcetypes alongside **Splunkbase 7777** tiles.

## Value

Binding **serialNumber**-level **`complianceStatus`** timelines to **ServiceNow** fabrics and **MSD** site ownership collapses ambiguous **`Pending Config`** noise after approved **`deploy`** batches versus stealth **`Out-of-Sync`** drift from **`mgmt0`** edits—Splunk retains immutable **`ISSU`**/**backup** histories longer than volatile **NDFC** UI panes while **`rest_ta`** parity checks expose silent HTTPS gaps behind enterprise proxies terminating **`Bearer`** automation tokens Heavy Forwarders renew quarterly.

## Implementation

Deploy **rest_ta** collectors or hardened Python modular inputs targeting **`/fabrics`**, **`/switches`**, **`/compliance-status`**, **`/networks`**, **`/vrfs`**, **`/policies`**, **`/jobs`**—stamp **`cisco:ndfc:compliance`**, **`cisco:ndfc:image_mgmt`**, **`cisco:ndfc:backup`**, **`cisco:ndfc:network`**, **`cisco:ndfc:vrf`**, **`cisco:ndfc:fabric`** plus optional **`cisco:ndfc:syslog`**/**HEC** hooks—normalize enums via **FIELDALIAS**, wire Splunkbase **7777** workspaces, schedule drift dashboards after **`MaintenanceWindow`** lookups suppress sanctioned **`Pending Config`** bursts.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• **Nexus Dashboard cluster** release train matching **NDFC 12.4.x** guidance—three masters plus workers sized per Cisco worksheets before onboarding fabrics spanning **VXLAN EVPN**, **MSD multi-site**, **Classic LAN**, **External Fabric**, **IPFM**, or **custom templates**.
• **Bearer authentication**: ND login (LDAP, SAML, local) mints tokens reused on **GET** paths such as **https://<nd-cluster>/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics** —never embed operator passwords inside scripted searches; rotate automation secrets beside **Splunk** credential stores on Heavy Forwarders running collectors.
• Install **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, Splunkbase **1546**) or Python modular collectors polling REST segments (/fabrics, /switches, compliance-status, networks, vrfs, image policies, /jobs)—throttle intervals (example **300–900** seconds) so worker pods avoid HTTP **429** storms during ISSU waves.
• Deploy **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (`Splunkbase 7777`) for packaged tiles beside sourcetypes `cisco:ndfc:fabric`, `cisco:ndfc:compliance`, `cisco:ndfc:image_mgmt`, `cisco:ndfc:backup`, `cisco:ndfc:network`, `cisco:ndfc:vrf`, optional `cisco:ndfc:syslog`, via **Splunk Connect for Syslog** relays where syslog ingress wins over HTTPS collectors.
• Forward **HEC** tokens only on hardened listeners—when **NDFC 12.3+** webhook hooks emit compliance deltas, stamp Authorization headers exactly as Splunk Help describes and keep **props.conf** LINE_BREAKER aligned to multiline JSON bundles.
• Map **Performance Manager** (**PM**) statistics feeds (**flow**, **interface**, **device**) into `cisco:ndfc:fabric` enrichment macros separate from **NDI** telemetry—PM stays inside **NDFC** service boundaries documented in **LAN Fabric Configuration Guide**.

Step 1 — Wire collectors for intent drift and lifecycle jobs
(1) **Fabric inventory sweep**: schedule GET …/fabrics JSON into **cisco:ndfc:fabric** capturing fabricName, fabricTemplate, switchCount, MSD attributes—mirror pagination tokens Cisco documents so Splunk rows stay deduped via fabric_key macros.
(2) **Compliance polling**: GET …/switches/{serial}/compliance-status fills complianceStatus using Cisco enums (**In-Sync**, **Pending Config**, **Out-of-Sync**, **Failed**, **NA**) plus Failure Reason text—carry serialNumber unchanged for spine/leaf lookups tied to CMDB racks.
(3) **Top-down overlays**: ingest /networks and /vrfs events into **cisco:ndfc:network** / **cisco:ndfc:vrf** so VLAN/L3VNIs queued behind pending pushes correlate with **Pending Config** counters during staged brownfield migrations from legacy DCNM baselines.
(4) **Image governance**: poll policies plus **cisco:ndfc:image_mgmt** rows referencing SUM schedules, ISSU Started, ISSU Pre-Check Passed, ISSU Image Loaded, ISSU Reload Initiated, ISSU Completed, ISSU Rollback Initiated strings extracted from /jobs payloads orchestrating modular supervisors (Nexus 9504, 9508 chassis contexts).
(5) **Backup posture**: classify Backup Started, Backup Completed, Backup Failed, Restore Initiated, Restore Validation Passed, Restore Applied narrative keys inside **cisco:ndfc:backup** alongside AAA snapshots—daily SCP/SFTP destinations must align with retention macros Finance audits.
(6) **POAP bootstrap**: DHCP-assisted Pre-Provisioning Created, Bootstrap Started, Bootstrap Completed, Bootstrap Failed, Switch Onboarded markers ride syslog + webhook mixes—keep VLAN scopes isolated per Cisco POAP guidance so TFTP paths reflect mgmt0 bootstrap chains without crossing lab VLANs accidentally.

Step 2 — Author SPL focusing on drift enums and ISSU signals
Paste SPL into Search, validate field coverage using `| head 20`, then promote to reports after **FIELDALIAS** hedges camelCase drift between **NDFC** minor releases.

```spl
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=network_operations OR index=cisco_operations)
(sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:compliance" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:image_mgmt" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:backup")
earliest=-24h
| eval serial_key=trim(coalesce(serialNumber, serial_number, switchSerial, deviceSerial))
| eval fabric_key=trim(coalesce(fabricName, fabric_name, fabric))
| eval ftpl=lower(trim(coalesce(fabricTemplate, templateName, fabric_template, "vxlan_evpn")))
| eval comp_lc=trim(coalesce(complianceStatus, compliance_status, switchCompliance, status))
| eval pend=case(comp_lc="Pending Config",1,1=1,0)
| eval oos=case(comp_lc="Out-of-Sync",1,1=1,0)
| eval insync=case(comp_lc="In-Sync",1,1=1,0)
| eval failed=case(comp_lc="Failed",1,1=1,0)
| eval na_sw=case(comp_lc="NA",1,1=1,0)
| eval issu_line=lower(trim(coalesce(eventName, workflowEvent, jobType, operationName)))
| eval issu_hit=if(match(issu_line,"issu"),1,0)
| eval sum_hit=if(match(issu_line,"software upgrade manager|sum_|image policy") OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:image_mgmt",1,0)
| eval backup_evt=lower(trim(coalesce(backupPhase, backupJobStatus, restorePhase, "")))
| eval backup_hit=if(match(backup_evt,"backup started|backup completed|backup failed|restore initiated|restore validation") OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:backup",1,0)
| where comp_lc IN ("In-Sync","Pending Config","Out-of-Sync","Failed","NA") OR issu_hit=1 OR sum_hit=1 OR backup_hit=1
| eval health_lane=case(
    oos=1 AND issu_hit=0,"drift_review",
    pend=1,"deploy_queue",
    failed=1,"failed_push",
    na_sw=1,"onboarding_gap",
    issu_hit=1,"issu_watch",
    backup_hit=1 AND isnull(comp_lc),"backup_restore_lane",
    insync=1,"aligned",
    1=1,"other")
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
        values(fabric_key) as fabrics,
        values(ftpl) as template_tags,
        max(pend) as has_pending,
        max(oos) as has_oos,
        max(failed) as has_failed,
        max(na_sw) as has_na,
        max(issu_hit) as issu_presence,
        max(backup_hit) as backup_presence,
        latest(comp_lc) as compliance_latest
    by serial_key, health_lane
| where health_lane!="aligned" OR has_oos=1 OR has_failed=1 OR has_pending=1 OR backup_presence=1
| sort -has_oos, -has_failed, -has_pending
| head 320
```

**Field rationale**
• **serial_key** collapses duplicates when collectors retry HTTP—dc() stays honest per physical switch rather than poll attempt IDs buried inside splunkd modular logs.
• **comp_lc** mirrors Cisco-published enums—never coerce **Pending Config** into drift until deploy_queue macros expire post-change windows documented with ServiceNow CHG IDs.
• **health_lane** separates legitimate ISSU choreography (issu_watch) from genuine drift_review rows referencing **Out-of-Sync** minus simultaneous ISSU strings—operators downgrade noisy lanes using lookup tie-ins to MaintenanceWindow tables.
• **template_tags** retains lowercase hashes (vxlan_evpn, msd, classic_lan, external_fabric, ipfm) so dashboards facet MSD gateway behaviours separately from standalone pods.

Step 3 — Validation rituals beside **NDFC** canvases
(1) Open **LAN Fabric → Fabric Switches**, highlight any **Failed** row—Splunk **Failure Reason** text must match GUI cells before paging automation trusts **deploy_failed** lanes.
(2) Compare **Pending Config** spikes against scheduled **top-down** pushes—**networks**/**vrfs** panes should show staged objects awaiting **deploy** acknowledgement identical to **pending_config** KPI tiles on **Splunkbase 7777**.
(3) Cross-read **ISSU** timelines inside **Switch Image Management** versus **ISSU Started** Splunk timestamps—skew larger than collector cadence implies Heavy Forwarder clock drift needing **NTP** fixes before blaming fabrics.
(4) Spot-check **NA** switches against inventory onboarding lists—often uninitialized **mgmt0** leases explain **NA** faster than phantom drift—confirm DHCP scopes per Cisco **POAP** docs before filing Sev tickets.
(5) Restore drills: **Restore Validation Failed** Splunk rows must match **Restore** wizard banners—capture screenshots into CMDB-linked tickets whenever **Restore Applied** fires during rehearsal weekends only.

Step 4 — Operational rhythm for fabric engineers
(1) Publish Splunk dashboards layering **has_oos** heatmaps atop **fabric_key** tiles plus **template_tags** splits between **VXLAN EVPN** versus **MSD** fabrics—rotate war-room TVs weekly during ISSU freeze periods.
(2) Tie **serial_key** rows to rack elevation lookups exported nightly—**has_na** backlog escalates DHCP administrators before fabric architects chase cabling spreadsheets alone.
(3) Pair **Backup Completed** nightly summaries with **Restore** tabletop exercises—immutable Splunk indexes satisfy auditors referencing **AAA** snapshots alongside **fabric-config** bundles stored off-cluster per Cisco retention bulletins.
(4) Align pager policies: **drift_review** pages only when **Out-of-Sync** persists beyond scheduled pushes; **deploy_queue** stays dashboard-only unless **Pending Config** ages past agreed minutes after **top-down** approvals.
(5) Document **SUM** pairing with golden NX-OS trains—**cisco:ndfc:image_mgmt** dashboards must cite change records whenever **ISSU Rollback Initiated** appears so leadership sees intentional retreat versus surprise faults.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting modes that pretend to be fabric drift
• **Collector clock skew** — last_seen lags fabric UI—fix chrony on Heavy Forwarder before accusing switches of stale In-Sync readings.
• **HTTP 401 storms** — expired Bearer tokens—rotate ND RBAC roles (NETWORK-ADMIN vs FABRIC-ADMIN) and refresh passwords.conf references without editing vendor default stanzas.
• **Pagination gaps** — partial /switches pages drop NA rows—implement looped GET with pageToken parameters described in **NDFC REST** guides before dedup hash macros misfire.
• **Webhook duplicates** — compliance webhooks replay identical serialNumber payloads—dedupe on serial_key + compliance_latest rather than raw _time alone.
• **Maintenance windows** — approved Pending Config states linger while operators lunch—suppress alerts using CHG macros referencing ServiceNow bridges.
• **Schema migration flashes** — upgrades from 12.2 to 12.3 temporarily mass-tag Pending Config until template migrations finish—pause paging until template_tags stabilize per migration runbooks.
• **ISSU false drift** — Out-of-Sync during ISSU Image Loaded is expected mid-window—route through issu_watch lane tied to eventName strings not generic drift macros.
• **SUM policy mismatch** — switch removed from policy yet Splunk still polls stale image rows—purge obsolete serialNumber from collector JSON maps nightly.
• **SCP backup path failure** — Backup Failed disk-quota strings often reflect disk—not fabric—confirm remote mount before fabric teams roll trucks.
• **VRF naming collisions** — cisco:ndfc:vrf duplicates from multi-tenant labs—scope indexes with fabric_key filters to prevent cross-customer top-down noise in shared service providers.
• **POAP DHCP collision** — competing DHCP servers in same VLAN emit Bootstrap Failed even when **NDFC** scope healthy—have network services inspect relays instead of fabric SMEs alone.
• **Manual emergency edits** — Out-of-Sync remains intentional after authorized NX-OS CLI fixes—suppress via CHANGE lookup referencing CAB exemptions until deploy reconciles intent.


## SPL

```spl
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=network_operations OR index=cisco_operations)
(sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:compliance" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:image_mgmt" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:backup")
earliest=-24h
| eval serial_key=trim(coalesce(serialNumber, serial_number, switchSerial, deviceSerial))
| eval fabric_key=trim(coalesce(fabricName, fabric_name, fabric))
| eval ftpl=lower(trim(coalesce(fabricTemplate, templateName, fabric_template, "vxlan_evpn")))
| eval comp_lc=trim(coalesce(complianceStatus, compliance_status, switchCompliance, status))
| eval pend=case(comp_lc="Pending Config",1,1=1,0)
| eval oos=case(comp_lc="Out-of-Sync",1,1=1,0)
| eval insync=case(comp_lc="In-Sync",1,1=1,0)
| eval failed=case(comp_lc="Failed",1,1=1,0)
| eval na_sw=case(comp_lc="NA",1,1=1,0)
| eval issu_line=lower(trim(coalesce(eventName, workflowEvent, jobType, operationName)))
| eval issu_hit=if(match(issu_line,"issu"),1,0)
| eval sum_hit=if(match(issu_line,"software upgrade manager|sum_|image policy") OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:image_mgmt",1,0)
| eval backup_evt=lower(trim(coalesce(backupPhase, backupJobStatus, restorePhase, "")))
| eval backup_hit=if(match(backup_evt,"backup started|backup completed|backup failed|restore initiated|restore validation") OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:backup",1,0)
| where comp_lc IN ("In-Sync","Pending Config","Out-of-Sync","Failed","NA") OR issu_hit=1 OR sum_hit=1 OR backup_hit=1
| eval health_lane=case(
    oos=1 AND issu_hit=0,"drift_review",
    pend=1,"deploy_queue",
    failed=1,"failed_push",
    na_sw=1,"onboarding_gap",
    issu_hit=1,"issu_watch",
    backup_hit=1 AND isnull(comp_lc),"backup_restore_lane",
    insync=1,"aligned",
    1=1,"other")
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
        values(fabric_key) as fabrics,
        values(ftpl) as template_tags,
        max(pend) as has_pending,
        max(oos) as has_oos,
        max(failed) as has_failed,
        max(na_sw) as has_na,
        max(issu_hit) as issu_presence,
        max(backup_hit) as backup_presence,
        latest(comp_lc) as compliance_latest
    by serial_key, health_lane
| where health_lane!="aligned" OR has_oos=1 OR has_failed=1 OR has_pending=1 OR backup_presence=1
| sort -has_oos, -has_failed, -has_pending
| head 320
```

## Visualization

(1) **Splunkbase 7777** fabric inventory tile filtered **`template_tags`** (**VXLAN EVPN** vs **MSD**). (2) Heatmap **`has_oos`**/**`has_pending`** by **`fabric_key`**. (3) Timechart **`ISSU`** workflow strings from **`cisco:ndfc:image_mgmt`**. (4) Table **`serial_key`**, **`compliance_latest`**, **`health_lane`**, **`fabrics`** for netops queues. (5) Single-value **`Backup Completed`**/**`Backup Failed`** ratio from **`cisco:ndfc:backup`**. (6) Map **`POAP`** events to DHCP scope owners for **`Bootstrap Failed`** drilldowns.
